Residential Stucco in Brazos County, TX

Brazos County is home to Bryan-College Station and a thriving residential market driven by Texas A&M University and the region's steady economic growth. Star Stucco of Austin installs residential stucco systems that give Brazos County homes a polished, durable exterior built for the Brazos Valley's heat and humidity.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Stucco for the Brazos Valley's Residential Market

Brazos County anchors the Brazos Valley, a region of rolling prairies, bottomland hardwoods, and the Brazos River. Bryan and College Station form the urban core, with a diverse housing stock that includes everything from historic Bryan neighborhoods to contemporary student-oriented developments and family homes in College Station's expanding south side.

The county's climate is slightly more humid than the Austin area, with warm, moisture-laden air flowing up from the Gulf. This humidity, combined with heavy clay soils and intense summer heat, demands stucco systems that manage moisture effectively and resist the mold and mildew pressure that comes with higher dew points.

Our team travels to Brazos County equipped for these conditions. We adjust our material selection, cure schedules, and moisture barrier specifications to match the Brazos Valley environment, ensuring your stucco performs as well here as it would anywhere in our Central Texas service area.

What's Included With Our Residential Stucco in Brazos County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Residential Exterior Assessment

We evaluate your home's walls, substrate condition, humidity exposure, and drainage to design a stucco system appropriate for Brazos County conditions.

Moisture Management & Prep

Given the Brazos Valley's humidity, we pay particular attention to weather-resistive barriers and ventilated drainage systems behind the stucco.

Lath & Scratch Coat

Galvanized metal lath is installed and a keyed scratch coat applied, providing a strong base for the system. Control joints are placed at critical points.

Brown Coat

The brown coat is applied, floated, and leveled to create the uniform surface needed for a quality finish coat application.

Finish Texture & Color

We apply the final coat in your selected texture with integral color or premium acrylic topcoats. Our materials resist mildew and UV in the Brazos Valley climate.

Cleanup & Documentation

The site is cleaned, the project is walked with you for approval, and warranty and maintenance documents are provided.

Common Residential Stucco Needs in Brazos County

Brazos County homeowners and builders trust us for residential stucco that handles the valley's unique climate and supports the area's growing housing market.

New Home Construction

Builders developing neighborhoods across College Station and Bryan choose stucco for its design range and proven performance in the Brazos Valley heat and humidity.

Rental Property Exteriors

The strong rental market near Texas A&M means exterior durability matters. Stucco is a landlord-friendly material that holds up to tenant turnover with minimal maintenance.

Full Exterior Renovation

Older Bryan homes with dated or damaged exteriors gain new life and better weather protection with a complete stucco installation.

Humidity-Resistant Systems

Brazos County's Gulf-influenced moisture levels require stucco systems with enhanced drainage and breathability. We use ventilated lath and proper weep details to manage this moisture.

Curb Appeal for Resale

A clean stucco finish increases a home's perceived value and attractiveness in Brazos County's active real estate market, where well-finished homes sell faster.

Why Choose Us for Residential Stucco in Brazos County?

  • Adapted methods for Brazos County's higher humidity and Gulf-influenced climate
  • Stucco systems with enhanced moisture management for Brazos Valley conditions
  • Clear, no-pressure inspections with documented findings
  • Professional crews experienced in both subdivision and rural Brazos County settings
  • Mildew-resistant, UV-stable finishes that hold up in humid Central Texas
  • Free estimates for homeowners across Brazos County

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Brazos County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Residential Stucco Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Get in Touch

Call (512) 706-9699 or submit a request online. We schedule regular visits to Brazos County and will find a time that works for you.

2

Evaluate Your Home

We inspect walls, drainage, humidity exposure, and soil conditions to develop a stucco plan matched to your property.

3

Review the Estimate

You receive a written proposal with detailed scope, materials, timeline, and transparent pricing.

4

Complete the Installation

Our crew executes the project on schedule, keeping you informed and presenting the completed work for your review.
